danger_signals.visual_form: Aggressive pitcher mechanics, varied ball trajectories
danger_signals.meaning: High challenge state; opponent is skilled and unpredictable

danger_signals.visual_form: Wind effects on ball trajectory, visible weather conditions
danger_signals.meaning: Match conditions are adverse; increased difficulty and unpredictability

danger_signals.visual_form: Pitch speed increase, narrower strike zone visual representation
danger_signals.meaning: Game difficulty biased toward pitcher; batter is in disadvantageous state

safety_signals.meaning: Fair match state; neither side has systematic advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear field conditions, no wind indicators
safety_signals.meaning: Predictable match conditions; reduced external variables

safety_signals.visual_form: Slower pitch animations, wider strike zone visual indicators
safety_signals.meaning: Game difficulty biased toward batter; player is in advantageous state

faction_indicators.faction: Real-world MLB teams (26 teams with real city names)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Team uniforms and jerseys reflecting real MLB visual identities
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposing team uniforms distinct from player-controlled team
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stadium-specific visuals and field layouts representing real MLB ballparks

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tommy Lasorda character model as spokesperson/endorsement figure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Marketing presence; celebrity athlete endorsement as game identity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Difficulty menu system; environmental hazard toggles; pitcher/batter bias selection interface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player adjusts game parameters; AI behavior difficulty scaling based on selected preset

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased pitch speed visual feedback; tighter strike zone representation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI pitcher throws faster; strikes harder to achieve; batter has reduced reaction window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visible slower pitch animations; wider strike zone indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI pitcher throws slower; easier to hit; batter has extended reaction window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ball trajectory curves; wind direction indicators on field
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ball path unpredictable; fielding positions must account for wind drift; pitch control becomes more challenging

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual indicators of fielder positioning; error animation sequences
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI-controlled fielders occasionally fail to catch; unpredictable defensive play; increased opportunity for safe hits

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Password system interface; league standings display; win/loss record tracking
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI teams maintain cumulative performance records; league standings evolve; World Series qualification determined by accumulated results

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of 26 real MLB teams features distinct ballpark visuals reflecting actual stadium architecture, communicating team identity through environmental design
Though player names are fictional, team rosters reflect statistical profiles of real teams (1989 NPB in Japanese version), creating authentic team identity through gameplay-relevant data
Ball trajectory, wind effects, and fielder positioning communicate environmental conditions and match state without explicit UI overlay
Player animations for batting, pitching, and fielding communicate skill level, fatigue state, and character ability through movement quality and speed

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard pitch speed and accuracy; moderate strike consistency
Increased throw velocity; higher accuracy; more aggressive pitch selection
Reduced pitch accuracy; visible slower animations; increased likelihood of wild pitches
More aggressive swing selection; wider strike zone engagement; higher contact rate
More conservative swing selection; tighter strike zone focus; reduced contact rate
Occasional failed catches; erratic ball pursuit; unpredictable defensive response
Reliable catch execution; consistent positioning; predictable defensive coverage
Ball trajectory curves unpredictably; visual drift indicators; fielder positioning becomes less reliable predictor of catch success

notes: Tommy Lasorda Baseball communicates game state primarily through mechanical difficulty adjustments and environmental variables rather than narrative or political context. World states are defined by pre-game configurations (difficulty mode, environmental hazards, error rates) that manifest in visual feedback (pitch speed, strike zone width, animation quality) and behavioral changes (AI aggression, fielder reliability). The 26 real MLB teams serve as faction indicators through visual differentiation (uniforms, stadium design) but lack explicit danger/safety signaling beyond gameplay mechanics. The game's communication paradigm is entirely mechanical—visual language expresses difficulty state and match conditions rather than narrative world-state. No political or faction conflict systems exist; all signaling is sport-mechanical.
